vocab.meaning: niềm yêu thích/sự quan tâm đến thực vật
vocab.example: Growing a small cactus on my desk was what first sparked my botanical interest when I was barely ten.
My father taught me how to propagate succulents from just a single leaf, which felt like magic to me as a child.
We spent the whole morning preparing nutrient-rich soil to ensure our vegetable patch would have the best start.

vocab.meaning: ý thức trách nhiệm chăm sóc/quản lý
vocab.example: Being tasked with watering the garden every evening instilled a strong sense of stewardship in me at a young age.
I was heartbroken when my marigolds began to perish after an unexpectedly harsh frost.

vocab.meaning: có tính trị liệu/giúp thư giãn tinh thần
vocab.example: I found the act of weeding and pruning surprisingly therapeutic, even amidst the stress of primary school exams.
We used to mix compost and other organic matter into the flower beds to help the roses bloom more vibrantly.

vocab.meaning: sự phát triển bị chững lại/còi cọc
vocab.example: One of my plants suffered from stunted growth because I had mistakenly placed it in a spot with almost no sunlight.
It was quite a sad sight to see the lilies wither away at the end of the season, though I knew they would return next year.
My grandmother was incredibly green-fingered; she could make almost any seedling thrive in the harshest conditions.
